name: cabextract
title: cabextract
description:
Microsoft Cabinet file unpacker
Cabextract is a program which unpacks cabinet (.cab) files, which
are a form of archive Microsoft uses to distribute their software
and things like Windows Font Packs.

commands.name: cabextract
commands.description: program to extract files from Microsoft cabinet (.cab) archives

arguments.description: Corrupted cabinet files will be 'fixed' to salvage whatever is possible from them. File
arguments.raw: -f  Corrupted cabinet files will be 'fixed' to salvage whatever is possible from them. File

arguments.description: Prints a page of help and exits.
arguments.raw: -h  Prints a page of help and exits.

arguments.description: Prompts before overwriting files.
Don't overwrite symlinks when extracting files.
arguments.raw: -k  Don't overwrite symlinks when extracting files.

arguments.description: When extracting cabinet files, makes each extracted file's name lowercase.
arguments.raw: -L  When extracting cabinet files, makes each extracted file's name lowercase.

arguments.description: Lists the contents of the given cabinet files, rather than extracting them.
arguments.raw: -l  Lists the contents of the given cabinet files, rather than extracting them.

arguments.description: Never overwrite existing files.
Files shall be extracted to standard output.
arguments.raw: -p  Files shall be extracted to standard output.

arguments.description: When extracting cabinet files, suppresses all messages except errors and warnings.
arguments.raw: -q  When extracting cabinet files, suppresses all messages except errors and warnings.

arguments.description: testing, listing or extracting cabinets which span multiple files, only cabinet
arguments.raw: -s  testing, listing or extracting cabinets which span multiple files, only cabinet

arguments.description: Tests the integrity of the cabinet. Files are decompressed, but not written to disk or
arguments.raw: -t  Tests the integrity of the cabinet. Files are decompressed, but not written to disk or

arguments.description: given alone on the command line, prints the version of cabextract and exits. Given
arguments.raw: -v  given alone on the command line, prints the version of cabextract and exits. Given

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cabextract because Microsoft Cabinet file unpacker.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cabextract as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cabextract  [-ddir] [-eencoding] [-f] [-Fpattern] [-h] [-i] [-k] [-l] [-L] [-n] [-p] [-q] [-s]` as documented upstream for cabextract.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cabextract with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cabextract --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Microsoft Cabinet file unpacker.
